reply_content: *deeply dead inside nostalgic sigh and facepalm* .... boy, life was sooooo much better in the 90s. It was literally like this all-encompassing universal childhood. Movie theaters had this irreplicable atmosphere that made you feel right at home and knowing that the projection running up in that booth was this elaborate analog mechanical art form was just this unexplainable wholesome soul-fulfillment that can't be replicated in any other form. And then the excitement of waiting for the DVD to come out wondering what juicy special features would be included and racing out to the store to rent/purchase it and half the profits from all theatrical and rental/purchase revenue went to the studios making it all fair and square; nowadays movie theaters are just these bland soulless luxury screening rooms, that glorious analog projectional art is gone and now you're just watching TV in public from a stupid LED projector and it's a soul-draining miserable waste of time. And nowadays whatever variants of DVD extras from the old days are mostly just uploaded to YouTube by the studios, sometimes before the movie even comes out. Now that modern technology has come of age I'm spoiled; I don't want the hassle of slipping a disc into a machine and then putting it away when I'm done watching, I just want to be able to access the whole package right on that state-of-the-art UHD all-in-one home system.
